pattern: Compute the sum gap, then greedily close it with the changes that move the most per operation, taking from the larger array's high values and the smaller array's low values.
hints: Only the difference between the two sums matters; figure out which array must grow and which must shrink.
Each element offers a fixed potential gain: in the larger array a 6 can drop to 1, in the smaller array a 1 can rise to 6.
Bucket all available gains, then spend them largest-first until the gap is non-positive; if the gains run out, it is impossible.

edge_inputs.description: impossible: long array of 1s vs single 6
single element each, max gap closable in one op

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    n2 = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    a = [rng.randrange(1, 7) for _ in range(n1)]
    b = [rng.randrange(1, 7) for _ in range(n2)]
    return [a, b]
